title: OS X/clang static member sizeof issue
body:
Trying to start with verilator on OS X Catalina (10.15.4)
Apple clang version 11.0.3 (clang-1103.0.32.59)
Target: x86_64-apple-darwin19.4.0
(I'm not even sure if this is a supported combo)

make test fails (and every other example fails as well) because of this:
 
```
g++  -I.  -MMD -I/Users/hp/src/verilator/test_regress/../include -I/Users/hp/src/verilator/test_regress/../include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=1 -faligned-new -fbracket-depth=4096 -fcf-protection=none -Qunused-arguments -Wno-parentheses-equality -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-shadow      -DVERILATOR=1 -DVL_DEBUG=1 -DTEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_vlt/t_a1_first_cc -DVM_PREFIX=Vt_a1_first_cc -DVM_PREFIX_INCLUDE="<Vt_a1_first_cc.h>" -DT_A1_FIRST_CC    -c -o verilated.o /Users/hp/src/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated.cpp
/Users/hp/src/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated.cpp:262:75: error: invalid use of member 'm_ser' in static member function
size_t Verilated::serialized2Size() VL_PURE { return sizeof(VerilatedImp::m_ser); }
                                                            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~
1 error generated.
```
I'm not sure why this 
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/b44efe7ef781fc140ae3279d937695363ab6802d/include/verilated.cpp#L262
would be invalid. Quick googling reveals that there used to be a bug in clang
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/35901577/is-it-illegal-get-sizeof-non-static-member-of-struct-nested-within-class-templ
which is similar to this issue.

Is it possible to work this around somehow?
Thanks,
Peter
